prompts.label: coffee scrub
prompts.query: Best coffee body scrub for cellulite and stretch marks
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body Original Coffee Scrub is excellent for cellulite and stretch marks due to its caffeine content and gritty texture that improves circulation. Pair it with moisturizer like Bondi Sands Body Oil for best results on these stubborn areas.
prompts.gptResponse: The best coffee body scrub for cellulite and stretch marks is Frank Body Original Coffee Scrub. It contains natural ingredients like coffee grounds and almond oil which help to improve circulation and hydrate skin.

prompts.label: exfoliating scrub
prompts.query: Which body scrub is best for removing dead skin and rough patches
prompts.claudeResponse: The Ordinary Natural Moisturizing Factors + HA combined with a mechanical scrub like Frank Body works well, or try Aesop Geranium Leaf Body Cleanser for gentle but effective dead skin removal. For rough patches, use 2-3 times weekly.
prompts.gptResponse: For removing dead skin and rough patches, consider the Tree Hut Shea Sugar Scrub in Coconut Lime. It combines sugar and shea butter to exfoliate and nourish your skin, leaving it smooth.

prompts.label: glycolic scrub
prompts.query: Best glycolic acid body scrub for keratosis pilaris and body acne
prompts.claudeResponse: Paula's Choice 10% Glycolic Acid Body Exfoliant is specifically formulated for keratosis pilaris and body acne without being too harsh. Follow with a soothing moisturizer like CeraVe Moisturizing Cream.
prompts.gptResponse: The best glycolic acid body scrub for keratosis pilaris and body acne is Paula's Choice Resist Weightless Body Treatment. It's formulated with glycolic acid and helps to smooth skin texture while fighting breakouts.

prompts.label: brightening scrub
prompts.query: Top body scrub for uneven skin tone and dark spots
prompts.claudeResponse: Use Aesop Geranium Leaf Body Cleanser paired with The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% serum to target uneven tone and dark spots. These complement each other for brightening results over 4-6 weeks.
prompts.gptResponse: Top body scrub for uneven skin tone and dark spots is the Neutrogena Body Clear Body Scrub. It contains salicylic acid to help brighten the skin and fight pigmentation.

prompts.label: body breakouts
prompts.query: Best body scrub for back acne and chest breakouts
prompts.claudeResponse: Salicylic acid body washes like Neutrogena Body Clear Acne Body Wash are clinically proven for back and chest acne. Follow with a non-comedogenic moisturizer like Cetaphil Pro Oil Control Moisturizer.
prompts.gptResponse: For back acne and chest breakouts, try the Murad Acne Body Wash. It contains salicylic acid and helps to clear pimples while preventing future breakouts.

prompts.label: natural scrub
prompts.query: Best natural coffee ground body scrub for sensitive skin
prompts.claudeResponse: Weleda Exfoliating Body Scrub or Burt's Bees Very Volumizing Pomegranate Conditioner (can be used on body) are gentler natural options for sensitive skin. Avoid Frank Body if your skin is reactive; these have fewer irritants.
prompts.gptResponse: Best natural coffee ground body scrub for sensitive skin is the Antipodes Coffee Bean Body Scrub. It uses gentle ingredients to exfoliate without irritating sensitive skin.

prompts.label: rough skin
prompts.query: How to get rid of rough bumpy skin on legs and arms
prompts.claudeResponse: Use a chemical exfoliant like The Ordinary Glycolic Acid 7% Solution 2-3 times weekly combined with intense moisturizing like Eucerin Advanced Repair Cream. This combination addresses both bumpy texture and dryness.
prompts.gptResponse: To get rid of rough bumpy skin on legs and arms, use Eucerin’s Roughness Relief Cream with Urea. It’s specifically designed to smooth rough skin while providing moisture.

prompts.label: body acne
prompts.query: What body scrub helps with bacne and chest breakouts
prompts.claudeResponse: Salicylic acid body washes such as Neutrogena Body Clear or Paula's Choice 2% BHA Body Spray are most effective for bacne and chest breakouts. Apply daily after showering for best results.
prompts.gptResponse: A great body scrub that helps with bacne and chest breakouts is the Acure Detoxifying Body Scrub. It’s formulated with charcoal and clay to cleanse and clear the skin.

prompts.label: keratosis pilaris
prompts.query: Best exfoliating scrub for chicken skin bumps on thighs
prompts.claudeResponse: AmLactin Daily Moisturizing Body Lotion with lactic acid is specifically designed for keratosis pilaris on thighs and arms. Use consistently for 2-3 weeks to see visible improvement in chicken skin bumps.
prompts.gptResponse: The best exfoliating scrub for chicken skin bumps on thighs is the First Aid Beauty KP Bump Eraser Body Scrub. It has a combination of glycolic and lactic acids that help reduce the appearance of bumps.

prompts.label: dull skin
prompts.query: How to brighten dull body skin with exfoliation
prompts.claudeResponse: Combine mechanical exfoliation like Frank Body Coffee Scrub with chemical exfoliation using The Ordinary Glycolic Acid, then apply a brightening moisturizer like Olay Regenerist. Do this 2-3 times weekly for glowing skin.
prompts.gptResponse: To brighten dull body skin with exfoliation, consider using the Dermalogica Exfoliating Body Scrub. It helps remove dead skin and promotes a brighter, more even skin tone.

prompts.label: where buy
prompts.query: Where to buy good coffee body scrubs in Australia
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body, Bondi Sands, and Sol de Janeiro are available at Chemist Warehouse, Priceline, and Sephora stores across Australia. Online, shop at Mecca, Sephora Australia, or the brands' official websites for full range.
prompts.gptResponse: You can find good coffee body scrubs in Australia from retailers like Sephora or at health food stores like Health Food Shop and Farmers Markets that sell local brands.

prompts.label: online purchase
prompts.query: Best place to order body exfoliating scrubs online Australia
prompts.claudeResponse: Mecca Australia and Sephora Australia offer the widest selection of premium body scrubs with fast delivery. For local brands, shop Frank Body and Bondi Sands directly through their websites for best pricing and stock.
prompts.gptResponse: For ordering body exfoliating scrubs online in Australia, check out Adore Beauty or Mecca, which carry a wide range of scrubs from various brands.

prompts.label: price range
prompts.query: How much should I spend on a quality body scrub
prompts.claudeResponse: A good quality body scrub ranges from $15-40 AUD; Frank Body costs around $20-25, which offers good value. Don't spend over $50 unless it's a luxury brand—effectiveness plateaus beyond this price point.
prompts.gptResponse: Generally, you should expect to spend between $10 to $30 on a quality body scrub depending on the brand and ingredients.

prompts.label: sol comparison
prompts.query: Frank body vs Sol de Janeiro body scrubs which is better
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body is grittier and more affordable ($20-25), while Sol de Janeiro offers smoother, creamier formulas ($35-45) with better fragrance. Choose Frank for strong exfoliation, Sol de Janeiro if you prefer gentler texture.
prompts.gptResponse: Between Frank Body and Sol de Janeiro body scrubs, it really depends on your preference for scent and texture, but Frank Body is known for its strong coffee fragrance and effective exfoliation.

prompts.label: ordinary comparison
prompts.query: Frank body coffee scrub vs The Ordinary body products
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body delivers superior exfoliation with visible texture results, while The Ordinary focuses on active ingredients like glycolic acid for chemical exfoliation. Use Frank for mechanical scrubbing, The Ordinary for deeper cellular renewal.
prompts.gptResponse: Frank Body Scrub offers a more natural approach with coffee grounds, while The Ordinary focuses on chemical exfoliants, so it depends on whether you prefer physical or chemical exfoliation.

prompts.label: bondi comparison
prompts.query: Frank body or Bondi Sands body care range
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body is more cost-effective ($20-25) with cult following for results, while Bondi Sands offers luxury body care with better moisturizing formulas ($30-35). Choose Frank for budget exfoliation, Bondi Sands for pampering experience.
prompts.gptResponse: Frank Body and Bondi Sands both have excellent products, but Frank Body is often favored for its coffee scrubs and unique appeal.

prompts.label: reviews
prompts.query: Are coffee body scrubs actually worth the hype on social media
prompts.claudeResponse: Coffee body scrubs have genuine benefits for circulation and texture, but social media hype is overstated—they're not miracle workers. They're effective as part of a routine but won't eliminate cellulite; results are modest and require consistent use.
prompts.gptResponse: Coffee body scrubs have gained popularity due to their exfoliating properties and potential to improve skin texture, making them worth trying if you're curious.

prompts.label: reddit opinions
prompts.query: Reddit reviews of popular Australian body scrub brands
prompts.claudeResponse: Australian Reddit users consistently recommend Frank Body for value, Bondi Sands for luxury feel, and AmLactin for clinical results on rough skin. Most agree coffee scrubs work best when combined with moisturizing and consistent use.
prompts.gptResponse: Reddit reviews often praise brands like Frank Body and Skinstitut for their effective body scrubs. Users appreciate their results and sensory experiences.

prompts.label: influencer products
prompts.query: Body scrubs influencers actually use vs just promote
prompts.claudeResponse: Influencers genuinely use Frank Body, Bondi Sands, and Sol de Janeiro regularly, but they also receive free products and may not disclose this. Look for influencers who show before/afters over months rather than quick promotions for authenticity.
prompts.gptResponse: Influencers often promote brands like Frank Body due to their popularity, but many genuinely use and endorse them for their effectiveness.

prompts.label: australian brands
prompts.query: Best Australian made body scrubs for dry climate
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body Original and Bondi Sands are Australian-made and excellent for dry climates because they don't over-strip moisture. Pair with heavy moisturizers like Sukin Hydrating Body Lotion for best results in harsh conditions.
prompts.gptResponse: For dry climates, consider using the Sukin Body Scrub, as it's formulated to hydrate and nourish the skin while exfoliating.

prompts.label: local stockists
prompts.query: Body scrub brands available at Chemist Warehouse Australia
prompts.claudeResponse: Chemist Warehouse stocks Frank Body, Bondi Sands, Neutrogena Body Clear, AmLactin, and CeraVe body products. Check their website for current stock, as availability varies by location.
prompts.gptResponse: Chemist Warehouse Australia carries several body scrub brands such as St. Ives, Neutrogena, and the Coconut and Coffee range from Frank Body.

prompts.label: aussie skincare
prompts.query: Top Australian skincare brands for body exfoliation
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body (Melbourne-based) and Bondi Sands (Sydney-based) are top Australian exfoliation brands, along with Sukin for natural options. All three offer quality products at reasonable prices and are stocked nationally.
prompts.gptResponse: Top Australian skincare brands for body exfoliation include Frank Body, Aspect, and Dr. LeWinn’s, known for their innovative formulations.

prompts.label: scrub alternatives
prompts.query: Alternatives to expensive body scrubs that actually work
prompts.claudeResponse: AmLactin Daily Moisturizing Body Lotion ($15-20) and The Ordinary Glycolic Acid ($8-10) deliver professional results at fraction of premium prices. Combine these two for clinical-grade exfoliation cheaper than luxury brands.
prompts.gptResponse: Affordable alternatives to expensive body scrubs include brands like The Ordinary or DIY scrubs using ingredients like sugar and coconut oil.

prompts.label: diy options
prompts.query: Homemade body scrub recipes vs store bought products
prompts.claudeResponse: Homemade scrubs (sugar, coffee, coconut oil) work initially but lack preservatives and active ingredients, making them less effective long-term. Store-bought like Frank Body are more consistent, hygienic, and contain additional skin-beneficial ingredients worth the cost.
prompts.gptResponse: Homemade body scrub recipes can be effective and customizable, but store-bought products are often more consistent and formulated for specific needs.

prompts.label: budget options
prompts.query: Cheaper alternatives to premium coffee body scrubs
prompts.claudeResponse: The Ordinary Glycolic Acid 7% ($10-12) paired with a basic sugar scrub offers similar results to Frank Body for half the price. Alternatively, AmLactin ($15-20) provides clinical exfoliation cheaper than most premium coffee scrubs.
prompts.gptResponse: Cheaper alternatives to premium coffee body scrubs include brands like Grin Natural Coffee Scrub, which offer similar benefits at a lower price point.

prompts.label: frank worth
prompts.query: Is frank body coffee scrub worth the money
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body at $20-25 offers excellent value given its effectiveness and longevity; one jar lasts 2-3 months with regular use. Yes, it's worth the money compared to luxury brands costing $40+, though cheaper alternatives exist for budget shoppers.
prompts.gptResponse: Frank Body Coffee Scrub is generally considered worth the money for its natural ingredients and effective exfoliation.

prompts.label: frank reviews
prompts.query: Frank body scrub honest reviews and results
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body users report visible improvement in texture, circulation (skin appears slightly flushed), and smoothness within 2-3 weeks of consistent use. Results are real but modest—it's a good exfoliant, not a cellulite cure, and works best combined with moisturizing.
prompts.gptResponse: Honest reviews of Frank Body Scrub often highlight its effectiveness in smoothing skin and its pleasant coffee scent.

prompts.label: frank products
prompts.query: Which frank body scrub is best for my skin type
prompts.claudeResponse: Frank Body Original suits most skin types; use Frank Body Coffee & Ginger for sensitive skin seeking gentleness, or Frank Body Cinnamon for those wanting extra warmth and circulation boost. Choose based on sensitivity level rather than skin type—all are similarly effective.
prompts.gptResponse: The best Frank Body scrub for your skin type is the Original Coffee Scrub for dry skin, while the Coconut Coffee Scrub is great for hydration.
